clinical_history: 51 year old woman with a dilated cardiomyopathy and atrial arrhythmias after chemotherapy for breast cancer. What is the rhythm?
diagnosis: This ECG shows classic flutter waves. The grouped beating (bigeminal) pattern here represents a repetitive variable block mechanism (2:1 and 4:1 conduction). For comparison see also Case #188. The ECG also shows nonspecific findings consistent with her cardiomyopathy (biventricular dilation and a markedly reduced ejection fraction): slow R wave progression V1-V3, borderline low limb lead voltage and diffuse precordial T wave inversions.
